tags.name: Account
tags.description: Account profile, settings, billing charges, credit history, call detail records, SIP registration status, and self-service signup and password recovery.

tags.name: ACL
tags.description: Manage the IP Access Control List for IP-based SIP authentication. Only routable public IPv4 CIDRs with masks /8, /16, /24, or /32 are accepted.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Manage the account SIP authentication type (Digest, IP Auth, or both) and password.

tags.name: e911
tags.description: Provision and manage e911 emergency service records for telephone numbers on the account.

tags.name: Gateways
tags.description: Manage SIP gateways (outbound trunks). System route types (USER, T30, VOICE_BRIDGE, DID, T38, RTC_BRIDGE, SDN) are always present and read-only.

tags.name: iNumbering
tags.description: Number ordering, search, porting, and messaging campaign provisioning.

tags.name: Lookups
tags.description: Per-lookup billed services. CNAM returns the registered Caller ID Name. LRN returns Location Routing Number and carrier details. Requires the service to be enabled on the account.

tags.name: Messaging
tags.description: SMS/MMS sending, routing, messaging campaigns, and delivery records.

tags.name: Numbers
tags.description: Manage telephone numbers: routing, forwarding, DNIS translation, inbound CNAM lookup, outbound Caller ID Name (LIDB), SMS routing, and fax-to-email.

tags.name: Support
tags.description: Support ticket management. Customers see only their own tickets. Administrators see all tickets.

bearerAuth.description: 32-character hexadecimal API key obtained by POSTing your username and password to /v2.2/account/api-key. Pass it as the Bearer token on every request. Re-fetch after any password change.

accountApiKeyRequest.description: Body for fetching the API key (account/api-key — public endpoint).

portOutPinUpdateRequest.description: Set the port-out PIN that future losing carriers must present to release this number.

data.description: Payload of the numbers list response operation.

status.description: Outcome of the numbers list response operation.

numbersListResponse.description: Response wrapper for the numbers List operation.

carrier.description: Outbound carrier id assigned to the number after binding (17 = path A, 19 = path B).

upstreamCnpId.description: CNP ID from the CSP sharing record. SFL9UTQ = path A, SB8TWLO = path B.

previousNetwork.description: Messaging path the number was bound to before this call — null if there was no prior binding, 'A' or 'B' if a conflict was detected.

previousNetworkCleared.description: True if a conflicting binding on the other path was successfully disabled to allow this assignment.

number.description: Ten-digit telephone number that was bound to the campaign.

network.description: Messaging path the campaign resolves to (from the CSP sharing record).

numberMessagingCampaignAssignData.description: Data payload for the number messaging campaign assign operation.

cnam.description: Whether inbound CNAM lookup is enabled for the number (in cnamData).

cnamData.description: Data payload for the cnam operation.

numberAddRequest.description: Request body for the number Add operation.

paid.description: Invoice fulfillment status. true = customer has paid the invoice for this credit. false = invoice is still outstanding.

date.description: Timestamp the credit was applied to the account.

zip.description: Postal code stored against the number.

dn.description: Eleven-digit telephone number bound to this e911 record (E.164 without the leading +, i.e. country code 1 prepended).

city.description: City name stored against the number.

address1.description: Street address line 1 stored against the number.

address2.description: Street address line 2 stored against the number (apartment, suite, floor) — may be an empty string.

callername.description: Caller name displayed to the PSAP dispatcher.

state.description: Two-letter US state code stored against the number.

e911Entry.description: e911 record bound to a telephone number. Returned by GET /v2.2/e911 (each item), GET /v2.2/e911/{dn}, POST /v2.2/e911, and PUT /v2.2/e911/{dn}. The internal sms field is removed before responding.

embedded.description: Contact details for the customer. Each subfield is an array of {id, value, type} objects (websites has just {id, value}).

photoUrl.description: Profile photo URL (may resolve to a gravatar or social network if photoType is set).

email.description: Primary email address on the customer profile.

supportCustomer.description: End-user profile attached to a support ticket. Most fields are optional and only populated when the customer provided them.

portUrl.description: URL to track port order status.

streetSuffix.description: Optional street direction suffix. Omit the field (or send an empty string) when none applies.

unitValue.description: Optional unit designator (e.g. APT, STE) and number.

streetType.description: USPS street type abbreviation (e.g. ST, AVE, BLVD).

pin.description: Port-out PIN. Required by many carriers especially mobile. Contact the losing carrier to obtain.

authPerson.description: Full name of the person authorized to sign the LOA.

features.description: Optional per-TN routing, LIDB, and SMS configuration applied after the port completes.

streetPrefix.description: Optional street direction prefix. Omit the field (or send an empty string) when none applies.

did.description: One or more 10-digit phone numbers to port. Toll-free (8xx) numbers are separated from geographic numbers automatically.

system.description: True for built-in system route types.

gateway.description: Gateway IP:port or system route name (USER, T30, etc.)

limit.description: Maximum concurrent calls. Null for system routes.

numbers.description: One or more numbers (max 100). Each may be a string or an object {number, route?}.

route.description: Optional gateway_id on this account; defaults to 4.

orderCreateRequest.description: Body for POST /v2.2/orders. numbers may be plain strings or objects with optional route.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent change to the ticket.

customerWaitingSince.description: How long the customer has been awaiting a reply.

customFields.description: Backend-defined custom fields. Each is {id, name, value, text} where text is a humanized rendering of value.

folderId.description: Folder the ticket currently sits in.

cc.description: Default CC addresses for outbound replies.

id.description: Ticket identifier — use as {id} in subsequent /support/tickets/{id} calls.

userUpdatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent staff update to the ticket.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp the ticket was created.

example.body: Hello, I cannot send SMS from my account.

embedded.description: Related records. Currently the v2.2 ticket endpoints always request only threads on the embed.

threads.body: Hello, I cannot send SMS from my account.

closedByUser.description: Full user record for the staff member who closed the ticket.

number.description: Human-readable ticket number shown to the customer.

threadsCount.description: Number of threads (messages and notes) on the ticket.

mailboxId.description: Mailbox routing — 1 for one brand, 2 for the others (see Handler_support::mailboxId).

preview.description: Short preview of the most recent customer message.
preview.example: Hello, I cannot send SMS from my account…

subject.description: Subject line as set by the customer or agent.

closedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp the ticket was closed.

state.description: Lifecycle state of the ticket record itself.

assignee.description: Staff user the ticket is currently assigned to — null if unassigned.

closedBy.description: User id of the staff member who closed the ticket (only set when status is closed).

status.description: Outcome of the support conversation operation.

createdBy.description: User or customer who created the ticket.

supportConversation.description: Full support ticket record. Returned by GET /v2.2/support/tickets/{id} and POST /v2.2/support/tickets.

coverage.description: Aggregated availability buckets matching the chosen dimension and filters.

inventoryCoverageData.description: Data payload for the inventory coverage operation.

via.description: Identifier of the author or sender.

source.description: How the message entered the system.

body.description: Message body (HTML for customer/agent messages, plain text for notes).
body.example: Hello, I cannot send SMS from my account.

ratingComment.description: Free-form comment that accompanies the rating.

rating.description: Customer satisfaction rating attached to the thread (1-5).

assignedTo.description: Staff user the parent ticket was assigned to when the thread was created.

action.description: System-generated action describing what changed (assignee changed, status changed, etc.). Present on system threads only.

state.description: Lifecycle state of the thread itself.

id.description: Thread identifier — unique within the ticket.

status.description: Outcome of the support thread operation.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp the thread was created.

createdBy.description: Author of the thread (a customer or a staff user).

type.description: Whether the message was sent by the customer, an agent (message), or is an internal note (visible to staff only).

openedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp the recipient first opened the message (email messages only).

supportThread.description: Single message (or system event) within a support ticket conversation.

text.description: Message body (UTF-8). Long messages may be split into multiple segments.

fromNumber.description: Source 10-digit number; must be on the authenticated account.

start.description: Echo of the `start` query parameter (Unix seconds — descending range start; newer than `end`).

end.description: Echo of the `end` query parameter (Unix seconds — descending range end).

cdr.description: Newest-first call records that fell within the requested start/end range.

key.description: Composite range key — [accountUsername, startEpochUnixSeconds]. Both are strings as emitted by the record store.

ip.description: IPv4 address that handled the call leg.

cn.description: URL-encoded display name resolved by CNAM at call time. URL-decode before showing to users.

nr.description: Nibble rate (dollars per minute) applied to this call.

ba.description: Billed amount in dollars. Equals roughly dur × nr / 60.

cid.description: Caller ID (calling party) — ten-digit telephone number.

id.description: Stable record identifier for the call.

gateway.description: Gateway address (IP[:port]) or system route name (in numberDetail).

translated.description: Destination digits after translation rewrite is applied.

forward.description: Whether call forwarding is enabled for the number.

forwardTo.description: Forwarding destination — ten-digit telephone number — or null when forwarding is disabled (in numberDetail).

smsEnabled.description: Whether the number has inbound SMS routing configured.

name.description: Name on the porting account at submission.

portDetail.description: Full detail for a single port-in record.

status.description: Outcome of the account cdr response operation.

accountCdrResponse.description: Response wrapper for GET /v2.2/account/cdr. Returns per-call billing records that fall within the requested start/end range, newest first. Each row carries a composite key of [account, startEpoch] so the caller can paginate by time.

sms.description: Admin only. Whether SMS messaging is enabled.

callerId.description: Default outbound caller ID — ten-digit telephone number (in accountPutRequest).

notify.description: Whether the account receives low-balance notifications (in accountPutRequest).

ccs.description: Admin only. Concurrent-call cap on the account.

intl.description: Admin only. Whether international calling is allowed.

notifyThreshold.description: Balance threshold (in dollars) below which notifications fire (in accountPutRequest).

e911.description: Admin only. Whether e911 provisioning is allowed.

mms.description: Admin only. Whether MMS messaging is enabled.

updated.description: Fields that were successfully updated before the conflict was raised.

value.description: Partially-applied new value for the field. Omitted when the field is sensitive.

message.example: Must be 6-10 alphanumeric characters with at least one letter and one number

example.message: Must be 6-10 alphanumeric characters with at least one letter and one number

status.description: Outcome of the auth put conflict response operation.

error.description: Error envelope for the auth put conflict response operation.

authPutConflictResponse.description: Returned when PUT /v2.2/auth completes some updates but rejects others (e.g. valid authType but invalid password). The successful subset is in `updated`; each rejection is in `errors` with the offending field and reason.

errors.message: Must be 6-10 alphanumeric characters with at least one letter and one number

pid.description: Short human-readable port reference shown to support.

authPutResponse.description: Response wrapper for the auth Put operation. Each entry in `updated` names the field that was changed; `value` is only present for fields whose new value is meaningful to echo (e.g. authType). Sensitive fields like password omit `value`.

addressid.description: Upstream address identifier — pass this to PUT /v2.2/e911/{dn} to provision the address for a number.

address2.description: Echoed/normalized street address line 2 — may be an empty string.

number.description: The number whose LIDB was updated.

cnam.description: The sanitised caller name written to local cache and the routing network (max 15 alphanumeric).

carrierStatus.description: Status returned by the LIDB network. 'Success' indicates the request was accepted; other values describe the failure. Local cache is updated regardless of this field.

messages.description: Message Detail Records (or Delivery Records when type=dlr) for this number/range, newest first.

key.description: Composite range key. For sms/mms: [destinationNumber, timestamp]. For dlr: [accountId, fromNumberWithCountryCode, timestamp].

rate.description: Billed rate per message (sms and mms only).

value.description: Per-record fields. Shape depends on the requested type.

services.description: Per-service feature flags. true means the service is enabled on this account.

notify.description: Whether the account receives low-balance notifications (in accountData).

notifyThreshold.description: Balance threshold (in dollars) below which notifications fire (in accountData).

prefix.description: Digits to prepend on outbound calls.

faxEnabled.description: True when inbound fax-to-email routing is configured for the number.

translated.description: Destination after translation rewrite — typically equal to `number` unless the account routes the DID through a translated extension.

forward.description: True when call forwarding is enabled for the number.

forwardTo.description: Forwarding destination when `forward` is true. Null when no forward is configured.

ticketResponse.description: Response wrapper for a single support ticket (GET /v2.2/support/tickets/{id} and POST /v2.2/support/tickets).

threads.body: Hello, I cannot send SMS from my account.

ticket.preview: Hello, I cannot send SMS from my account…
ticket.subject: Cannot send SMS

lata.description: Local Access and Transport Area code.

inventoryItem.description: A single telephone number available for assignment.

info.description: VoiceTel REST API. All resource identifiers are in the URL path. Authenticate every request with `Authorization: Bearer <apikey>`. To obtain your API key, POST `{"username": <id>, "password": "<password>"}` to /v2.2/account/api-key — this is the only endpoint that does not require an Authorization header. The response is `{"status":"success","data":{"apikey":"<32-hex-string>"}}`. Re-fetch the API key after any password change. Rate-limited endpoints: account, account/recurring-charges, account/cdr, account/api-key — 6 requests per hour per IP.
info.title: VoiceTel API

post.description: Places an the number order for the listed numbers and adds them to the authenticated account. Charges accounts.cash by accounts.did × len(numbers). Supports the Idempotency-Key header for safe retries (the cached response is returned for 24h on replay).

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Client-supplied opaque token. Retries with the same key return the cached response and do NOT re-charge or re-order.

requestBody.description: Numbers to order. Each may set an optional route (gateway_id on this account, default 4).

502.description: the network rejected the order; no DB writes performed

402.description: accounts.cash insufficient for the order total

400.description: Validation failed before any carrier or DB write

post.description: Creates a new sub-account. Requires administrator credentials. The new account password is auto-generated and returned.

put.description: Updates account settings. Customers may update: timezone, notify, notifyThreshold, callerId. Administrators may additionally update: ccs, service flags, and rate fields.

400.description: Bad request response from account put.

get.description: Returns full account details including balance, services, and rates. Rates are read-only. Rate limited: 6 requests per hour per IP.

delete.description: Removes a gateway. Will fail if DIDs are currently routed to it.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Resource identifier — used by gateways delete.

404.description: Not Found response from gateways delete.

409.description: Cannot remove — DIDs still routed to it

204.description: No Content response from gateways delete.

parameters.description: Resource identifier — used by gateways put.

404.description: Not Found response from gateways put.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Resource identifier — used by gateways get.

404.description: Not Found response from gateways get.

post.description: Validates a street address for e911 provisioning. Returns the validated address including an addressid for use with PUT /e911/{dn}.

requestBody.description: Body for validate an e911 address.

post.description: Assigns a telephone number to the account. Account balance must cover the monthly DID rate.

get.description: Returns all telephone numbers on the account with full detail including route, gateway, forwarding, CNAM, SMS, and fax configuration.

404.description: No numbers found

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Ten-digit telephone number — used by numbers fax delete.

204.description: No Content response from numbers fax delete.

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Ten-digit telephone number — used by numbers fax set.

post.description: Submits a number port-in. Replicates the legacy LNP web form: creates a port record, opens a support ticket, notifies the LNP team, and deducts $6 per TN from accounts.cash. Numbers must have been confirmed portable via GET /v2.2/ports/availability/{number} within the prior hour. The LOA must be uploaded after submission.

requestBody.description: Address, account, and TN list for the port-in submission.

400.description: Validation failed before any side effect

data.message: Port request a3a2a submitted. Please complete the LOA to proceed.

201.description: Port record created and support ticket opened

get.description: Returns every port-in record submitted by the authenticated account, newest first.

401.description: Authentication failed for the port list

ports.message: Your numbers will be porting on May/04/2026 at 10:00 AM Central.

200.description: Port records owned by this account

get.description: Returns the complete credit history for the account — all credits regardless of invoice status. Each entry includes a paid flag: true means the customer has fulfilled the invoice; false means the invoice is still outstanding. Use /v2.2/account/credits/paid or /v2.2/account/credits/unpaid to retrieve a filtered subset.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter results to credit entries with the given paid/unpaid state.

get.description: Returns the full detail for one port-in record. Returns 404 if the id does not exist or is owned by a different account.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Port record identifier (5-character alphanumeric pid) from the list response's pid field.

404.description: No port record matches that id under this account

parameters.name: dn
parameters.description: Ten-digit telephone number for the e911 record (DELETE /v2.2/e911/{dn}).

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Range start as Unix timestamp. Defaults to now.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Range end as Unix timestamp. Defaults to 24h ago.

get.description: Returns up to `quantity` numbers matching the tnMask and optional filters. Results are cached for 10 minutes.

parameters.description: Prefix or wildcard pattern; `201`, `201555`, `201555*`.

parameters.name: quantity
parameters.description: Maximum results, 1–1000. Defaults to 10.

parameters.name: province
parameters.description: Limit results to this 2-letter US state or Canadian province code.

post.description: Sends a text message. If mediaUrls is provided it goes as MMS via the messaging network; otherwise SMS via the SMS routing routed by the source number's assigned network. Charges the account on success.

409.description: Messaging not enabled on this account, this number, or the recipient has opted out

get.description: Returns SMS or MMS Message Detail Records for a single number within a timestamp range. Both endpoints of the range are inclusive.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: sms (default), mms, or dlr. dlr returns delivery status events for sent messages.

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Ten-digit telephone number on the authenticated account — used by message history.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Unix timestamp range start (newer). Defaults to now.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Unix timestamp range end (older). Defaults to 24h ago.

delete.description: Removes each listed number from the given campaign. The campaign itself is unchanged; only the number-to-campaign bindings are removed. campaignId is required — the system uses CSP sharing to resolve which messaging path holds the bindings, then issues path-appropriate removal calls.

parameters.name: numbers
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of 10-digit telephone numbers to remove from the campaign. Maximum 1000.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: TCR campaign id the numbers are being removed from. The network is resolved via CSP sharing.

delete.summary: Unassign multiple numbers from a campaign

409.description: Campaign is not on a supported messaging path

get.description: Returns the Location Routing Number (LRN) and carrier details for the destination number. Billed per lookup.

put.description: Sets the outbound Caller ID Name (CNAM/LIDB) for the number. Writes the name to the local cache (used by the softswitch for outbound caller-id) and then submits the update to the LIDB network. The local cache update is durable; a downstream failure is reported via the response's carrierStatus field but does not fail the request. Max 15 alphanumeric characters.

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Ten-digit telephone number — used by numbers lidb.

requestBody.description: Body for set outbound caller id name (lidb).

404.description: Number not found or not on this account (numbers forward remove).

401.description: Missing or invalid bearer token (numbers forward remove).

400.description: Field Required: cnam (outbound caller name, max 15 alphanumeric characters).

get.description: Returns all PayPal payments received on the account, newest first. Each entry includes the amount, date, PayPal transaction ID, payment status, and the payer email address. Payments represent real cash received; for credit adjustments see GET /v2.2/account/credits.

post.description: Returns the current API key for the account identified by username and password. No Bearer token is required or accepted — credentials must be provided in the request body. Rate limited to 6 requests per hour per IP.

patch.description: At least one of routeIn / routeOut must be present. routeIn must be a numbers_sms row id owned by the authenticated account, or 0 to detach.

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Ten-digit telephone number on the authenticated account — used by number messaging patch.

404.description: Not Found response from number messaging patch.

403.description: routeIn does not belong to this account

get.description: Returns the current inbound route, outbound carrier, and enabled state for this number.

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Ten-digit telephone number on the authenticated account — used by number messaging get.

get.summary: Read messaging state for one number

404.description: Not Found response from number messaging get.

post.description: Releases the number back to the network (different from removing it from the account). A release event is created; the number cannot be re-assigned.

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Ten-digit telephone number — used by number release.

post.summary: Release a number back to the network

get.description: Returns all message threads for a ticket. Customers can only view their own tickets.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Resource identifier — used by support ticket messages.

404.description: Ticket not found (support ticket messages).

401.description: Account row not found (support ticket messages).

messages.body: Hello, I cannot send SMS from my account.

200.description: OK response from support ticket messages.

403.description: Forbidden response from support ticket messages.

delete.description: Removes this number from the given campaign. The campaign continues to exist with its other bindings. campaignId is supplied as a query parameter (per REST: DELETE should not carry a request body).

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Ten-digit telephone number on the authenticated account.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: TCR campaign id the number is being removed from.

delete.summary: Unassign a number from a campaign

404.description: Number not on this account

put.description: Binds the number to a registered campaign at the network. Number must be on the authenticated account.

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Ten-digit telephone number on the authenticated account — used by number messaging campaign assign.

requestBody.description: Campaign id to bind to this number.

put.summary: Assign a 10DLC campaign to a number

404.description: Not Found response from number messaging campaign assign.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Resource identifier — used by tickets delete.

404.description: Not Found response from tickets delete.

204.description: No Content response from tickets delete.

put.description: Updates the status of a ticket. Customers can only update their own tickets.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Resource identifier — used by tickets put.

404.description: Ticket not found (tickets put).

get.description: Returns a ticket including its threads. Customers can only view their own tickets.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Resource identifier — used by tickets get.

404.description: Not Found response from tickets get.

threads.body: Hello, I cannot send SMS from my account.

ticket.preview: Hello, I cannot send SMS from my account…
ticket.subject: Cannot send SMS

patch.description: Updates the four-digit PIN that future losing carriers must present to release this number. Requires that the number is currently on the authenticated account.

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Ten-digit telephone number owned by the authenticated account — used by number port out pin update.

requestBody.description: Four-digit PIN to set on this number for future port-out validation.

patch.summary: Set the port-out PIN on a number

404.description: Number not on the authenticated account

post.description: Sends a password recovery link to the email address on file. No authentication required.

404.description: Email not found

post.description: Creates a new account. No authentication required. Returns account credentials and API key. A verification email is sent.

requestBody.description: Body for create a new account.

400.description: Bad request response from account create.

post.description: Creates a new support ticket. The customer email is automatically populated from the authenticated account. Administrators may optionally specify a different email.

example.subject: I need help with my account

requestBody.description: Body for create a support ticket.

threads.body: Hello, I cannot send SMS from my account.

ticket.preview: Hello, I cannot send SMS from my account…
ticket.subject: Cannot send SMS

get.description: Returns support tickets. Customers see only their own tickets. Administrators see all tickets.

401.description: Missing or invalid bearer token (or the authenticated account row is missing).

502.description: Support service error (the underlying ticketing backend rejected the request).

threads.body: Hello, I cannot send SMS from my account.

tickets.preview: Hello, I cannot send SMS from my account…
tickets.subject: Cannot send SMS
